# terrible_community_general_ovh_ip_failover (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `application_key` (String) The applicationKey to use.
- `application_secret` (String) The application secret to use.
- `consumer_key` (String) The consumer key to use.
- `endpoint` (String) The endpoint to use (for instance `ovh-eu`).
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `name` (String) The IP address to manage (can be a single IP like `1.1.1.1` or a block like `1.1.1.1/28`).
- `service` (String) The name of the OVH service this IP address should be routed.

### Optional

- `timeout` (Number) The timeout in seconds used to wait for a task to be completed. Default is 120 seconds.
- `wait_completion` (Boolean) If `true`, the module waits for the IP address to be moved. If false, exit without waiting. The `taskId` is returned in module output.
- `wait_task_completion` (Number) If not `0`, the module waits for this task ID to be completed. Use `wait_task_completion` if you want to wait for completion of a previously executed task with `wait_completion=false`. You can execute this module repeatedly on a list of failover IPs using `wait_completion=false` (see examples).
